summary: Frontend-only E2E testing of the Alessandri-inspired Vargas y Zúñiga redesign. Validated 73 functional checks across hero rotator, language toggle/persistence, sticky header, full nav smooth-scroll, news (featured+thumbs), studio split panel, 22 practice-area items, experience, team carousel+detail, recent news, dual CTA bands (red+orange), contact section, footer with back-to-top, mobile menu, embedded chat widget integration (vyz-widget-panel.vyz-open), and Emergent badge hiding. 72/73 passed (98.6%). No console errors. Chat widget opens correctly via cta-chat. ES is default; EN switch and localStorage persistence verified.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking [data-testid="back-to-top"] the page did NOT reach the top within 800ms (window.scrollY=419). Smooth-scroll may simply be slow, but worth verifying duration / using behavior:'smooth' with sufficient time, or confirm scrollIntoView target. Likely cosmetic timing only.

action_items: Optional: verify back-to-top scroll behavior reaches scrollY=0 reliably (may just need longer animation budget or set behavior:'auto' fallback).
critical_code_review_comments: Hero auto-rotates every 8s with intervals — ensure cleanup on unmount to avoid memory leaks (verify useEffect return cleanup).
openChat() polls #vyz-widget-launcher up to 12×300ms (3.6s); if widget script fails to load (CDN down) there is no user-visible fallback message — consider a graceful UX state.
All 22 practice-area items hard-coded in both ES and EN; consider centralizing in a single source if items grow.
Language persisted via localStorage 'vyz-lang' — works on reload as tested.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All data-testid selectors from the review_request exist and are wired. Chat widget loads from external script (~1-3s); waited up to 3.5s for vyz-open class. ES default + EN toggle works, persists via localStorage. Mobile menu shows 6 nav items at 390x844. Only minor: back-to-top scroll may need slightly more time to reach 0; not a functional defect.
rca of the issue: back-to-top: scrollY measured 419 after 800ms wait. Smooth scroll from a deep footer position can take >800ms in some browsers. Either increase wait in test, or in code use a shorter animation/scrollTo({top:0, behavior:'smooth'}) which Chromium implements in ~600-900ms depending on distance. Not a bug per se.
